expectations.text: Identifies the work as belonging to the Deployment & Domains area of the platform
expectations.evidence: Discusses custom-domain finalization but never locates it in the platform's component structure; gives generic 'Rails/Django/Node service' advice.

expectations.text: Recommends modeling the flow as a resumable state machine that commits each transition (Principle 16), not one blocking function
expectations.evidence: Lines 36-37: 'Model finalization as distinct steps driven by background jobs and the domain's status column'; recommends guarded state transitions.

expectations.text: Recommends the domain move through explicit persisted intermediate states that can resume after a crash
expectations.evidence: Recommends statuses pending -> dns_pending -> dns_verified -> gsc_pending -> active and 'Persist status transitions, not just the final value' (line 63).

expectations.text: Recommends running the slow external GSC verification work asynchronously off the request path (Principle 19)
expectations.evidence: Lines 48-49: 'let the queue hold the wait, not a thread'; enqueue jobs rather than block.

expectations.text: Recommends bounding the verification retries with backoff and an explicit terminal state (Principle 21)
expectations.evidence: Line 61: 'Bounded retries + a terminal failure state' with a max attempt count.

expectations.text: Explicitly advises against the user's proposed single blocking finalize_domain function as written
expectations.evidence: Line 3 'the part I'd push back on'; line 80 'don't let it be a synchronous function that blocks on Google.'

expectations.text: Grounds the advice in the appsmith-v2 principles catalogue and cites specific principle numbers
expectations.evidence: No principle numbers; explicitly generic — 'I don't have your actual source', 'Rails/Django/Node service, etc.'
